error.signature: Could not load dynamic library 'libcudart.so.12'; dlerror: libcudart.so.12: cannot open shared object
error.regex: (Could not load dynamic library|libcuda(rt|nn|blas)|cannot open shared object|CUDA.*library.*not found|dlerror)

verdict.summary: TensorFlow cannot find CUDA runtime libraries. CUDA version mismatch or LD_LIBRARY_PATH not set.

dead_ends.action: Install the latest CUDA toolkit regardless of TF version
dead_ends.why_fails: TF requires specific CUDA versions. TF 2.15 needs CUDA 12.2, not 12.5 or 11.x.

dead_ends.action: Set LD_LIBRARY_PATH in the Python script
dead_ends.why_fails: LD_LIBRARY_PATH must be set BEFORE Python starts; setting it inside Python has no effect on dlopen

dead_ends.action: Symlink the wrong CUDA version to the expected filename
dead_ends.why_fails: ABI differences between CUDA versions cause crashes; symlinks hide version mismatches

workarounds.action: Install the exact CUDA version matching your TF version
Use pip install tensorflow[and-cuda] for automatic CUDA bundling
workarounds.how: pip install tensorflow[and-cuda]  # bundles compatible CUDA/cuDNN

workarounds.action: Use conda which manages CUDA dependencies automatically
workarounds.how: conda install tensorflow-gpu  # installs matching cudatoolkit
